q: Fluid is a substance that offers no permanent _______ to deforming forces.
Fluid is a substance that offers no permanent resistance to _______ forces.
The study of fluids has two parts, the statics and the _______.
The fluid _______ concentrates on the fluids at rest.
The fluid _______ concerns with the fluid in motion.
The study of the flow of liquid is called _______.
The study of liquid at rest is called _______.
The relation between the volume and the pressure of a gas obeys _______ law.
The relation between the volume and the temperature of a gas obeys _______ law.
The volume of a liquid does not depend on the pressure if the _______ is constant.
In the flow of the fluids, the path or the flow of the fluid particles is called _______.
If the fluid particles are moving steady in smooth paths in layers, it is a _______ flow.
If each layer of the fluid moves smoothly past the adjacent layers with no mixing, it is a _______ flow.
In _______ flow, the streamlines do not cross each other.
If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is irregular, it is _______ flow.
If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is always changing their directions, it is _______ flow.
If the flow or the path of the fluid particles is always whirling, it is _______ flow.
In _______ flow, the speed of the fluid at a point is continuously undergoing changes in both the magnitude and the direction.
The internal friction in the fluids is called _______.
For ideal laminar fluid flow, the viscosity is _______.
The flow of an ideal fluid flow is a _______ flow.
The equation, A₁v₁=A₂v₂ is the continuity equation for _______ fluid flow.
The speed of water spraying from a hose increases by making the hose area _______.
The speed of water spraying from a hose decreases by making the hose area _______.
Bernoulli's equation can be derived using the _______ principle to a fluid flowing in a tube.
If the volume flow rate through x cm diameter pipe is 2 m³s⁻¹, the volume flow rate through 2x cm diameter of the same pipe is _______.
The difference in the _______ of the airflow causes a pressure difference above and below the wing of a flying plane.
The pressure above the wing of a flying plane is _______ than the pressure below the wing.
The difference in pressures above and below the wing of a flying plane provides a _______ on the wing.
When the piston of the sprayer is pressed, the fast moving air causes a _______ pressure at the tip of the nozzle.
When the piston of the sprayer is pressed, the liquid inside the container is at a _______ pressure.
The _______ effect is a particular manifestation of Bernoulli's theorem.
The _______ effect is commonly associated with a spinning object moving through a fluid.
The ball spinning through the air drags some of the _______ around it.
The deflection of the spinning ball passing through a fluid due to different pressures is known as the _______ effect.
The resistance to fluid motion is analogous to _______ when a solid moves on a surface.
The resistance to fluid motion is called _______.
The internal resistance of the fluid is called _______.
Viscosity arises when there is _______ motion between layers of the fluid.
The _______ measures the resistance to the flow arising due to the internal friction between the fluid layers as they slip past one another when fluid flows.
The viscosity of liquids _______ rapidly with an increase in temperature.
The viscosity of gases _______ with an increase in temperature.
The SI unit of viscosity is _______.
The unit of viscosity other than pascal second is _______.
There are _______ ways to measure the fluid's viscosity.
The _______ viscosity is the fluid's resistance to flow due to an external force.
The _______ viscosity is the resistive flow of a fluid due to gravity.
The _______ viscosity is also known as absolute viscosity.
The shear stress is the _______ force per unit area.
The _______ is the ratio of the shearing stress to the velocity gradient.
Stokes' law expresses the drag force resisting the fall of small spherical objects through a fluid medium under the influence of _______.
Surface tension is the tendency of the surface of a liquid to behave like a stretched _______.
Surface tension is due to the attractive forces between liquid molecules called _______.
_______ refers to forces between molecules of the same substance.
_______ makes water molecules pull together making the water surface like an elastic skin.
_______ is the reason a water strider can stride along the water surface without sinking.
The SI unit of the surface tension is _______.
The surface tension of the liquid is the _______ between liquid molecules.
Capillary action is the spontaneous flow of a _______ into a narrow tube or porous material.
Cohesion and adhesion are two types of _______ forces.
_______ refers to forces between molecules of the same substance.
_______ refers to forces between molecules of different substances.
_______ forces pull the liquid into the capillary tube.
For wicking to occur, a tube needs to be sufficiently _______ in diameter.
_______ action helps bring water up into the roots.
For a glass tube with a bore of about _______ mm in diameter, the water will rise to a height of about _______.
Liquids that rise in small bore tubes inserted into the liquid are said to _______ the tube.
Capillarity does not occur for _______ liquids.
When a glass tube is dipped into water, the _______ between glass and water causes a thin film of water to be drawn up over the inner and outer surfaces of the tube.
Adhesion refers to forces between molecules of _______ substances.
Capillary action helps bring _______ up into the roots.
Capillary action is caused by the combination of the cohesive and the _______ forces.
When a glass tube is dipped into water, the _______ causes water to be drawn up.
